etymology.description: semantic 水(水) (shuǐ, "water") + phonetic 相(相) (xiāng) -> Xiang River
etymology.interpretation: The character 湘(xiāng) is formed by combining the semantic component 水(shuǐ, "water"), denoting a river or water-related context, with the phonetic component 相(xiāng), which provides the pronunciation. This combination specifically refers to the Xiang River in China, indicating a water body by its name.

examples.chinese: 湘江蜿蜒流过城市和田野(湘江蜿蜒流過城市和田野)。
examples.pinyin: Xiāng jiāng wānyán liú guò chéngshì hé tiányě.
examples.english: The Xiang River winds through cities and fields.

examples.chinese: 许多人来到湘江边野餐(許多人來到湘江邊野餐)。
examples.pinyin: Xǔduō rén lái dào Xiāng jiāng biān yěcān.
examples.english: Many people come to the banks of the Xiang River for a picnic.

examples.chinese: 湘水之上，舟影摇曳(湘水之上，舟影搖曳)。
examples.pinyin: Xiāng shuǐ zhī shàng, zhōu yǐng yáoyè.
examples.english: On the Xiang waters, boat shadows flicker.
